# Jade

> Native macOS terminal workspace — project tabs, split panes, Git worktrees, command palette, Rich Input + Obsidian capture, local Ollama AI. SwiftUI + libghostty (Metal).

## Summary

Jade is a free, open-source macOS terminal multiplexer. Organize shells by project with persistent tabs, splits, and worktrees. Primary capture is **Rich Input** (`⌘I`) and Obsidian MCP; structured **project session logs** use the palette + vault templates. Built-in editor/VCS are fallback surfaces; deep editing belongs in your IDE.

## Audience

- macOS developers who live in the terminal
- Users juggling multiple repos and worktrees
- Local-first AI (Ollama) without cloud lock-in
- Obsidian users exporting session notes from the shell

## Key features (core)

- Project-based terminal workspaces: sidebar tabs, split panes, persistence
- Git worktrees and light Git status in the sidebar
- Command palette (⌘K): files, actions, MCP tools, project log, dev shortcuts
- Rich Input capture, snippets (general/project), Ollama AI assistant panel
- Project session logs (`.jade/`, todo/goals) → Obsidian via MCP
- `jade` CLI: open projects, agent notification hooks

## Optional / frozen (maintenance-only)

- Built-in editor, file tree, VCS tab — quick peek; prefer external IDE
- AI usage panel — Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor CLI only
- WebSocket remote server — disabled by default; protocol frozen until a client ships
- Moltis agent gateway — dev-only build flag; not default production path
- Voice dictation, natural-language shell commands, remote SSH spaces

## Requirements

- macOS 14+
- Swift 6.0+ to build from source

## Install

- Build from source: https://github.com/dot-RealityTest/jade#local-development
- Releases (when published): https://github.com/dot-RealityTest/jade/releases
- Website: https://dot-realitytest.github.io/jade/

## Keywords

macOS terminal, terminal multiplexer, SwiftUI terminal, libghostty, Ghostty, project-based terminal, command palette, Ollama, local AI, Obsidian MCP, Git worktrees, developer workspace, split panes, snippets, open source

## Lineage

Fork/evolution of Muxy (https://github.com/muxy-app/muxy). UX inspiration from cmux project-level attention cues. User-facing name: Jade; internal compatibility: Muxy bundle id and paths. **No Jade mobile app in this repo.**

## Privacy

Terminal content, vault paths, and AI prompts stay on your Mac unless you opt into SSH remote spaces or the LAN WebSocket server. No account required.
